statements.content: Displaying the name or number of selected files
Show the name when single file is selected.
Display the number of files when multiple items are selected.

> Displaying the name or number of selected files
> Displaying an error message
> If additional action is required for the file you want to upload
No applicable data was found
Displaying an error message
Display the validation results and guide the user to reselect the file.
This file is not valid. Please select another file.

Exception!
If the validation result must be clearly recognized by the user, an error message may be displayed in the modal dialog. Since the modal dialog stops the user's task flow, use it only when it's necessary.
If the validation result must be clearly recognized by the user, an error message may be displayed in the modal dialog. Since the modal dialog stops the user's
task flow, use it only when it's necessary.
This file is already selected. Please select another file.

Exception!
When the user selects the same file as the already uploaded file, the options such as  overwrite may be provided in a pop-up dialog.
When the user selects the same file as the already uploaded file, the options such as  overwrite may be provided in a pop-up dialog.
Select file to load the data.
If additional action is required for the file you want to upload
If you need only some of the selected file data, a data table UI for viewing items should be provided using a pop-up dialog.

1. Use a local browser and make the user's task flow concise.
2. Provide a validation check (or duplication check) if necessary right after the user selects a file.
3. Display an error message as inline text.
4. If a user's additional action is required after selecting a file, a Client UI such as a pop-up dialog can be added to the flow.
The user can upload a single or multiple files to the client app to complete the task.
Last updated: Dec 6, 2022
